name: Commonwealth of Independent States
climate: Continental to subarctic across most of the zone; arid and semi-arid in Central Asia; Mediterranean pockets in the Caucasus

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: Western neighbor and former primary energy customer; EU sanctions reshape CIS trade patterns

neighbors.name: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation
neighbors.relevance: Russia and Central Asian CIS members are SCO participants; security and economic alignment with China

neighbors.name: Black Sea Region
neighbors.relevance: Russia-Ukraine conflict zone; grain and energy exports flow through Black Sea ports

neighbors.name: Arctic Sea Route
neighbors.relevance: Russia controls the Northern Sea Route; LNG and resource extraction drive Arctic development

neighbors.name: Baltic Sea Region
neighbors.relevance: Kaliningrad exclave and Russian Baltic coastline create NATO-Russia friction zone

neighbors.name: Belt and Road Initiative
neighbors.relevance: Central Asian CIS states are key BRI transit corridors; Chinese infrastructure investment is growing

links.description: Sanctions regime and energy decoupling following Russia-Ukraine conflict; Moldova seeks EU accession
Russia and Central Asian members align with China on security and counter-Western economic frameworks
Russia, Kazakhstan, and Azerbaijan coordinate oil production under OPEC+ agreements
Russian and Ukrainian grain and energy exports historically flow through Black Sea ports
Russia develops the Northern Sea Route for LNG and resource shipments to Asia
Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan serve as BRI land corridors linking China to European markets
Caspian energy pipelines connect Azerbaijan and Kazakhstan to global markets via Gulf-adjacent routes
Finland and Norway share borders with Russia; NATO expansion has heightened security tensions
